headline: The social measures tech leaders put forward to address AI's destructive potential sound familiar in Europe
description: Aware of the potentially devastating impact their technology could have on jobs and society, the leaders of OpenAI and Anthropic are advancing a range of progressive ideas aimed at preserving social cohesion.

headline: Energy transition becomes more accessible to the French middle class
Iran says it sent response to US peace proposal via Pakistan
'There are no innocents in Gaza': Channel 14, the Israeli television channel accused of genocidal rhetoric
description: The channel, whose ratings have been steadily increasing, gives prominence to the most violent rhetoric, including calls for the 'annihilation' of the Palestinian territory. Three Israeli NGOs have compiled a detailed record of these statements, to which Le Monde has had access, and have filed a petition before the Supreme Court.

headline: Inside Saint Petersburg's Immortal Regiment and its shift from tribute to propaganda
description: During the May 9 commemorations, Russian photographer Mary Gelman walked the streets of Vladimir Putin's hometown to take the pulse of a country sealed off from the world.

headline: How much space should cities give to wild vegetation?
description: Bordeaux's new city council has launched a major program to weed sidewalks across the city, while in Montpellier, officials are considering expanding what it calls France's first 'wild district.'

headline: 'The word 'masculinism' is deeply inappropriate and depoliticizes feminism'
description: Philosopher Geneviève Fraisse challenges, in an interview with Le Monde, certain words used to discuss issues related to the status of women that have spread through public discourse, in both activist circles and everyday language.

Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
description: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

headline: Leo XIV's return to realism and formalism at the Vatican
description: One year after his election as head of the Catholic Church, Pope Leo, Francis's successor, stands out from the Argentine pontiff in his approach, yet maintains a through line on substance.

headline: 'Germany lacks breakthrough innovation'
description: According to the German budgetary policy specialist, the industrial model that has prevailed in Germany since 1945 does not sufficiently focus on cutting-edge technologies and is more threatened by Chinese competition than ever.

headline: 'Israel's impunity in Gaza can only encourage it to be just as ruthless in Lebanon'
